2604.19245.title: Talking to a Know-It-All GPT or a Second-Guesser Claude? How Repair reveals unreliable Multi-Turn Behavior in LLMs
2604.19245.paper_summary: Repair, an important resource for resolving trouble in human-human conversation, remains underexplored in human-LLM interaction. In this study, we investigate how LLMs engage in the interactive process of repair in multi-turn dialogues around solvable and unsolvable math questions. We examine whether models initiate repair themselves and how they respond to user-initiated repair. Our results show strong differences across models: reactions range from being almost completely resistant to (appropriate) repair attempts to being highly susceptible and easily manipulated. We further demonstrate that once conversations extend beyond a single turn, model behavior becomes more distinctive and less predictable across systems. Overall, our findings indicate that each tested LLM exhibits its own characteristic form of unreliability in the context of repair.
2604.19245.paper_summary_zh: 修復，作為解決人與人之間對話中問題的重要資源，在人與大型語言模型（LLM）互動中仍然未被充分探索。在本研究中，我們調查了LLM如何在關於可解和不可解數學問題的多輪對話中參與互動修復過程。我們檢視模型是否會主動發起修復，以及它們如何回應用戶主動發起的修復。我們的結果顯示模型之間存在明顯差異：反應範圍從幾乎完全抵抗（適當的）修復嘗試到高度易受影響且容易被操控。我們進一步證明，一旦對話超過單一輪次，模型行為變得更加獨特且在系統之間的可預測性降低。總體而言，我們的發現表明，每個測試過的LLM在修復的背景下都表現出其自身特有的不可靠性形式。
2604.19245.author: Clara Lachenmaier et.al.
2604.19245.authors: Clara Lachenmaier, Hannah Bultmann, Sina Zarrieß
